sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.16 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: {a)} Does the set of (not necessarily boundedly) Engel elements of a group without elements of order~2 form a subgroup? [r]{b)} The same question for torsion-free groups. [r]{c)} The same question for right-ordered groups. [r]{d)} The same question for linearly ordered groups. There are examples of 2-groups where a product of two (unboundedly) Engel elements is not an Engel element. { V.\,V.\,Blud...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
